text.title: go-backend Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-backend as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: gin.Default() already includes Logger+Recovery; agents often add them again, double-logging every request.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin the middleware list explicitly and avoid default(). Only add what you need.

failure-mode.description: c.ShouldBindJSON returns Go validator errors that are user-unfriendly; agents ship them raw to the client (info-leak + bad UX).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Wrap validator errors in a standardized response type before sending. Never expose internal error types to the client.

failure-mode.description: LLMs love to spawn goroutines without ctx — every go func() you accept must include either a context check or a clear lifetime contract; otherwise the worker leaks forever.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every goroutine must have an exit path. Use context.Done() or close() to signal shutdown. Test for goroutine leaks with `go test -race`.

failure-mode.description: Agents export types from internal/ by mistake by referencing them in pkg/ — Go won't compile, but the agent's first fix is usually to move the type to pkg/.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force agents to fix the import direction instead. internal/ must stay unexported. Use interfaces at boundaries. The import direction rule is not negotiable — it prevents public/private boundaries from collapsing.

failure-mode.description: The provided Wrap(err, msg) returns 500 even for known categories — agents call it on user errors and lose the right HTTP status.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require typed wrappers (apperror.AsValidation, apperror.AsNotFound) that preserve the original error type and status code. Generic wrapping loses type information and results in poor client UX.

failure-mode.description: gin.Default() already includes Logger+Recovery; agents often add them again, double-logging every request.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin the middleware list explicitly and avoid default(). Only add what you need. Global middleware duplication leads to verbose logs and confusion about what's running.

failure-mode.description: Missing c.Next() in middleware breaks the handler chain — subsequent middleware and handlers don't run.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every middleware must call c.Next() to proceed. Forgetting this is a silent failure; the handler never runs.

failure-mode.description: Spawning goroutines in handlers without tracking them via WaitGroup — workers leak forever on shutdown.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every goroutine must be tracked and have an exit signal. Use context cancellation or WaitGroup to ensure graceful shutdown.
